chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Welcome to the CSS Grid masterclass in the Sigma Web Development course.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: CSS Grid Layout is a two-dimensional layout system for the web.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: It lets you lay out items in rows and columns simultaneously.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: To get started, you define a container element as a grid with display grid.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The grid-template-columns property defines the number and size of columns.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The grid-template-rows property defines the number and size of rows.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The fr unit represents a fraction of the available space in the grid container.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: For example, grid-template-columns 1fr 1fr 1fr creates three equal columns.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The gap property sets the gaps between rows and columns.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Grid-column-start and grid-column-end can be used to span items across multiple columns.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The shorthand grid-column lets you specify start and end in one property.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Grid-area is a shorthand for grid-row-start, grid-column-start, grid-row-end, grid-column-end.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The repeat function allows you to repeat columns or rows with a specific pattern.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The minmax function allows you to set a minimum and maximum size for a track.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Auto-fit and auto-fill are powerful values for creating responsive grids without media queries.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Grid template areas let you name grid areas and place items using those names.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: This makes your CSS very readable and easy to maintain.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Let me show you a practical example of creating a complete page layout with grid.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: We will create a header, sidebar, main content, and footer layout.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Define the grid template areas as header header, sidebar main, footer footer.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Now assign each element to its grid area using the grid-area property.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: CSS Grid is perfect for creating complex layouts with very little code.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The difference between Grid and Flexbox is that Grid is two-dimensional while Flexbox is one-dimensional.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: In practice, you will use both Grid and Flexbox together in your projects.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Grid for the overall page layout and Flexbox for components within the layout.

chunks.title: CSS Grid - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Practice both of these layout methods as they are essential for modern web development.

text: Welcome to the CSS Grid masterclass in the Sigma Web Development course. CSS Grid Layout is a two-dimensional layout system for the web. It lets you lay out items in rows and columns simultaneously. To get started, you define a container element as a grid with display grid. The grid-template-columns property defines the number and size of columns. The grid-template-rows property defines the number and size of rows. The fr unit represents a fraction of the available space in the grid container. For example, grid-template-columns 1fr 1fr 1fr creates three equal columns. The gap property sets the gaps between rows and columns. Grid-column-start and grid-column-end can be used to span items across multiple columns. The shorthand grid-column lets you specify start and end in one property. Grid-area is a shorthand for grid-row-start, grid-column-start, grid-row-end, grid-column-end. The repeat function allows you to repeat columns or rows with a specific pattern. The minmax function allows you to set a minimum and maximum size for a track. Auto-fit and auto-fill are powerful values for creating responsive grids without media queries. Grid template areas let you name grid areas and place items using those names. This makes your CSS very readable and easy to maintain. Let me show you a practical example of creating a complete page layout with grid. We will create a header, sidebar, main content, and footer layout. Define the grid template areas as header header, sidebar main, footer footer. Now assign each element to its grid area using the grid-area property. CSS Grid is perfect for creating complex layouts with very little code. The difference between Grid and Flexbox is that Grid is two-dimensional while Flexbox is one-dimensional. In practice, you will use both Grid and Flexbox together in your projects. Grid for the overall page layout and Flexbox for components within the layout. Practice both of these layout methods as they are essential for modern web development.
